task_yaml.task:
I want to grab the original high-resolution version of a famous NASA image
programmatically, plus its metadata — straight from NASA, not from a random
blog.

Start at NASA's image library site:

    https://images.nasa.gov/

This site is backed by a public API. Please find that API and use it (not by
scraping the gallery web UI) to:

- search for the "Pillars of Creation" image,
- pick the most relevant result and report its NASA id and title,
- then resolve that item's asset manifest to find the actual original /
  largest image file URL (the asset list is a second call, separate from the
  search results),
- download that original image and confirm it really is an image (content
  type + that the file is non-trivially large).

Save the metadata + the chosen original asset URL to
`/tmp_workspace/results/nasa_pillars.json`, save the downloaded image under
`/tmp_workspace/results/`, and save the exact request URLs you called plus
their raw JSON responses to `/tmp_workspace/results/nasa_evidence/`.
